text.title: sql-optimization Core Rules
text.summary: 9 testable rules grounding the sql-optimization methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Measure before optimizing. Use EXPLAIN ANALYZE (with BUFFERS) to understand actual plans, not guesses.
rule.rationale: Without measurement, optimization targets the wrong queries.

rule.statement: Optimize the right queries. Focus on frequent + resource-heavy queries, not just slow ones.
rule.rationale: Total impact beats individual query time.

rule.statement: Indexes are not free. Estimate write cost vs read benefit before adding one.
rule.rationale: Over-indexing degrades writes without guaranteed read gain.

rule.statement: Reduce data movement. Filter early (WHERE on largest table first), avoid Cartesian products, fetch only needed columns.
rule.rationale: Moving data between tables and across the network is the dominant cost.

rule.statement: For composite indexes, order columns by selectivity (most selective first) for equality; put equality columns BEFORE range columns.
rule.rationale: Optimizer can use the index for all conditions only if order matches the predicate shape.

rule.statement: Use covering indexes (INCLUDE clause) to make index-only scans possible.
rule.rationale: Without INCLUDE, the index scan must look up the table to fetch non-indexed columns.

rule.statement: Update statistics (ANALYZE / ANALYZE TABLE) after bulk loads or schema changes; stale stats produce bad plans.
Use connection pooling with pre-ping + recycle; stream large result sets rather than loading into memory.
rule.rationale: Connection-setup overhead and OOM on full result loads are the most common ops failure modes.

rule.statement: Skip when the input does not satisfy the Applies-If preconditions or another methodology owns this output shape.
rule.rationale: Prevents duplicate work and wrong-tool application.
